name: Klenk Law
description_short: Boutique Philadelphia-area estate planning law firm specializing exclusively in wills, trusts, probate, and estate administration across PA, NJ, NY, and FL.
description_long:
Klenk Law is a specialized estate planning law firm based in the Greater Philadelphia metropolitan area, founded and led by Peter L. Klenk. The firm operates as a boutique practice with intentional focus—handling only estate law matters rather than pursuing a broad general practice. They serve clients across Pennsylvania, New Jersey, New York, and Florida, with particular depth in Pennsylvania and New Jersey intestacy and probate law.

The firm offers comprehensive estate planning and probate services including will preparation, trust creation and administration, probate proceedings, estate settlement, and complex tax and asset protection strategies. They provide both single-issue assistance and full-service estate management. Services include document drafting, estate administration, trust administration, and guidance on Pennsylvania Intestacy Rules and New Jersey Surrogacy matters. The firm maintains detailed online resources including a glossary, blog, podcasts, and questionnaires to help clients understand estate planning processes.

Klenk Law distinguishes itself through transparent, fixed pricing; convenient scheduling with phone and Zoom options; specialized expertise in estate law only (not general practice); and a veteran-owned business model offering 20% discounts to veterans, first responders, and teachers. Their team of 13+ attorneys and staff emphasizes clear explanation of complex tax concepts and asset protection techniques. Customer testimonials consistently highlight ease of communication, professionalism, and the firm's ability to simplify complicated estate matters.

The primary limitation is that this firm handles estate planning and probate exclusively—they do not offer bankruptcy, business law, litigation, real estate transactions, or other legal services. They are geographically concentrated on the Northeast with service in four states. While pricing is described as "fair and transparent," specific fee schedules are not published on the website, requiring consultation. The firm's specialization is intentional and represents their business model rather than a weakness.
pros: Exclusive focus on estate planning and probate law creates deep specialized expertise versus generalist firms
Transparent, competitive fixed pricing model with no hidden fees (pricing details available upon consultation)
Veteran-owned business with 20% discount for veterans, first responders, and teachers
Multiple communication options including phone, Zoom, and in-person meetings for convenience
Comprehensive online resources including estate planning questionnaires, intestacy guides, and educational podcast
Large experienced team of 13+ attorneys and staff with individual attorney bios and specializations listed
Consistently strong customer reviews across multiple testimonials praising professionalism and clear communication
cons: Does not handle bankruptcy, litigation, business law, or other legal matters—not a full-service firm
Specific fee schedules not published online; requires direct contact for pricing information
Service area limited to four states (PA, NJ, NY, FL) with primary focus on Pennsylvania and New Jersey
Website content focuses on marketing and testimonials rather than detailed service descriptions or case examples
No information provided about response times, average case duration, or typical costs for common services
best_for: Individuals in the Philadelphia metro area seeking estate planning and will preparation with specialized expertise
Families needing probate and estate administration assistance in Pennsylvania or New Jersey following a death
Veterans, first responders, and teachers seeking estate planning with professional discount pricing
High-net-worth individuals requiring complex trust structures and tax/asset protection strategies
diagnosis: Klenk Law is ideal for individuals and families in the Northeast seeking specialized, focused estate planning and probate services from attorneys with deep expertise in this practice area. The main caveat is that this is exclusively an estate planning firm—anyone needing bankruptcy, litigation, business formation, or other legal services must seek counsel elsewhere. Their geographic service area (PA, NJ, NY, FL) also means they are not accessible to clients in other regions.
services: Tax and asset protection planning strategies
Estate planning consultation and questionnaire process
New Jersey surrogacy and estate law guidance
